title: Introduction to SEQuential
text: assumptions must met avoid unintended errors using SEQuential. : User provided time.col begins 0 per unique id.col entries, also assume column contains integers continues 1 every time step. e.g. (0, 1, 2, 3, …) allowed (0, 1, 2, 2.5, …) (0, 1, 2, 4, 5, …) . Provided time.col entries may order sort enforced beginning function, e.g. (0, 2, 1, 4, 3, …) valid begins 0 continuously increasing increments 1, even though ordered. eligible column names provided excused.cols binary (0/1) flag variables (respect time.col)

title: Introduction to SEQuential
text: R script, always start defining options object, SEQopts() helper. many defaults allow target exactly like change analysis. wiki specific pages dedicated causal contrast parameters affect , simplicity let’s start intention--treat analysis 20 bootstrap samples. general, options form {option}.{parameter} - may notice use bootstrap.nboot indicating parameter affects bootstrap
code: library(SEQTaRget)  options <- SEQopts(km.curves = TRUE, #asks the function to return survival and risk estimates                    bootstrap = TRUE, #asks the model to perform bootstrapping                    bootstrap.nboot = 20) #asks the model for 20 bootstrap samples

title: Introduction to SEQuential
text: next step running primary R function, SEQuential(). give options, data, data-level information. provide small simulated datasets test . provide print statements help track SEQuential() function processing given point time.

code: library(SEQTaRget) data <- SEQdata  model <- SEQuential(data, id.col = "ID", time.col = "time",                     eligible.col = "eligible", treatment.col = "tx_init",                      outcome.col = "outcome",                      time_varying.cols = c("N", "L", "P"),                     fixed.cols = "sex",                     method = "ITT",                     options = SEQopts(km.curves = TRUE))  outcome(model)                       # Returns a list of all outcome models as S3 fastglm objects over the course of bootstrapping diagnostics <- diagnostics(model)    # Returns a list of diagnostic tables diagnostics$outcome.unique[[1]]      # Returns a table of unique outcomes diagnostics$outcome.nonunique[[1]]   # Returns a table of nonunique outcomes  # Plotting km_curve(model)[[1]]                 # Returns the Survival curve km_data(model)[[1]]                  # Returns a dataframe of survival data in long-format for other analysis/plotting

title: Parameter Builder for SEQuential Model and Estimates — SEQopts
text: bootstrap Logical: defines SEQuential() run bootstrapping, default FALSE bootstrap.nboot Integer: number bootstraps, default 100 bootstrap.sample Numeric: percentage data use bootstrapping, [0, 1], default 0.8 bootstrap.CI Numeric: defines confidence interval bootstrapping, default 0.95 (95% CI) bootstrap.CI_method Character: selects way calculate bootstraps confidence intervals ("se", "percentile"), default "se" cense String: column name additional censoring variable, e.g. loss--follow-cense.denominator String: censoring denominator covariates right hand side formula object cense.eligible String: column name indicator column defining rows use censoring model cense.numerator String: censoring numerator covariates right hand side formula object compevent String: column name competing event indicator covariates String: covariates right hand side formula object data.return Logical: whether return expanded dataframe weighting information, default FALSE denominator String: denominator covariates right hand side formula object deviation Logical: create switch based deviation column deviation.col, default FALSE deviation.col Character: column name deviation deviation.conditions Character list: RHS evaluations length treat.levels deviation.excused Logical: whether deviations excused deviation.excused_cols, default FALSE deviation.excused_cols Character list: excused columns deviation switches excused Logical: case censoring, whether excused condition, default FALSE excused.cols List: list column names treatment switch excuses - length, ordered treat.level expand.Logical: TRUE, SEQuential() returns expanded data.table immediately expansion skips weighting, outcome modelling survival/risk steps. Useful need expanded dataset (e.g. inspect store separately). Default FALSE fastglm.method Integer: decomposition method fastglm (0L-column-pivoted QR, 1L-unpivoted QR, 2L-LLT Cholesky, 3L-LDLT Cholesky), default 2L glm.package Character: package use fitting GLMs, either "fastglm" (default) "parglm". "parglm" selected nthreads option controls number threads passed parglm::parglm.fit(). realistic SEQTaRget workloads (expanded datasets approximately million rows) "fastglm" faster; "parglm" may help substantially larger datasets parallel chunking outweighs setup overhead. parglm.control control object parglm::parglm.control() pass parglm::parglm.fit(). used glm.package = "parglm". Defaults parglm::parglm.control(method = "FAST"). encounter chol(): decomposition failed error (e.g. near-singular model matrices large datasets), pass parglm.control = parglm::parglm.control(method = "LAPACK") use numerically stable QR decomposition instead, switch using fastglm backend. followup.class Logical: treat followup class, e.g. expands every time indicator column, default FALSE followup.include Logical: whether include 'followup' 'followup_squared' outcome model, default TRUE followup.max Numeric: maximum time expand , default Inf (maximum) followup.min Numeric: minimum follow-time since trial enrollment include, must non-negative, default 0 followup.spline Logical: treat followup natural cubic spline (splines::ns()), default FALSE followup.spline.df Integer: degrees freedom passed splines::ns() followup.spline = TRUE. df = k, ns() places k - 1 interior knots quantiles followup. Must >= 1; df = 1 equivalent linear term generally want. Default 4 (3 interior knots). hazard Logical: hazard error calculation instead survival estimation, default FALSE indicator.baseline String: identifier baseline variables covariates, numerator, denominator - intended override indicator.squared String: identifier squared variables covariates, numerator, denominator - intended override km.curves Logical: Kaplan-Meier survival curve creation data return, default FALSE multinomial Logical: whether expect multilevel treatment values, default FALSE ncores Integer: number cores use parallel processing, default one less system max, see parallelly::availableCores() nthreads Integer: number threads use data.table processing, default data.table::getDTthreads() numerator String: numerator covariates right hand side formula object parallel Logical: define SEQuential process run parallel, default FALSE plot.colors Character: Colors output plot km.curves = TRUE, defaulted ggplot2 defaults plot.labels Character: Color labels output plot km.curves = TRUE order e.g. c("risk.0", "risk.1") plot.subtitle Character: Subtitle output plot km.curves = TRUE plot.title Character: Title output plot km.curves = TRUE plot.type Character: Type plot create km.curves = TRUE, available options 'survival' (default), 'risk', 'inc' (case censoring) risk.times Numeric vector: follow-times (data's follow-units) report risk difference risk ratio km.curves = TRUE. requested time snapped latest available follow-. final follow-time always included. Default NA reports final follow-time. seed Integer: starting seed selection.first_trial Logical: selects first eligible trial expanded dataset, default FALSE selection.prob Numeric: percent total IDs select selection.random, bound [0, 1], default 0.8 selection.random Logical: randomly selects IDs replacement run analysis, default FALSE subgroup Character: Column name stratify outcome models survival.max Numeric: maximum time survival curves, default Inf (maximum) treat.level List: treatment levels compare, default c(0, 1) trial.include Logical: whether include 'trial' 'trial_squared' outcome model, default TRUE visit String: column name visit indicator variable, e.g. "visit" visit.denominator String: visit denominator covariates right hand side formula object visit.numerator String: visit numerator covariates right hand side formula object weight.eligible_cols List: list column names indicator columns defining weights eligible weight models - order treat.level weight.lower Numeric: IPCW weights truncated lower bound, must non-negative, default 0 weight.lag_condition Logical: whether weights conditioned treatment lag value, default TRUE weight.p99 Logical: forces weight truncation 1st 99th percentile weights, override provided weight.upper weight.lower weight.preexpansion Logical: whether weighting done pre-expanded data, default TRUE weight.upper Numeric: weights truncated upper end weight, default Inf weighted Logical: whether perform weighted analysis, default FALSE

title: SEQTaRget v1.4.2
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-21 Remove mention units time docs. Improve memory usage bootstrapping. Fix --one labeling survival output followup = k correctly represents survival k intervals, adding row followup = survival.max + 1 final interval’s estimate. Fix expansion bug subjects experiencing outcome early incorrectly carried forward outcome=0 rows subsequent periods truncating trial first event row Add expand.option SEQopts(). TRUE, SEQuential() returns expanded data.table directly skips analysis steps, users want inspect store expanded dataset . Fix followup.spline = TRUE basis genuinely non-linear. Splines now built model formula via splines::ns() instead applied single-column transform followup, new followup.spline.df option (default 4) controls number basis functions. treatment--followup interaction now uses spline basis. Knots baked full expanded followup fit time basis identical fit prediction time across bootstraps survival grids. Internally, formula column extraction now uses .vars(), user-supplied covariates may include ns(), bs(), (), factor(), poly() etc. without breaking expansion. Rename format.time() format_time() wasn’t S3 method hence causing roxygen2 write incorrect information helpfile. Add package level helpfile bump roxygen2 8.0.0. Add parglm alternative GLM fitting backend. Add warm starts bootstrap GLM fits. Add dataset size summary verbose output. Fix selection.random propagated SEQopts() internal parameters. Cap data.table 2 threads tests vignette builds, skip multisession parallel test CRAN, comply CRAN’s 2-core policy checks. Apply SEQopts(nthreads = ...) setting data.table SEQuential(). Previously used parglm backend ignored default serial fastglm path, data.table ran global default thread count. previous global setting restored call finishes. Add risk.times option SEQopts(). km.curves = TRUE, risk difference risk ratio (CIs) reported requested follow-time, just end follow-. Requested times snapped latest available follow-, final time always included. risk.comparison risk.data tables gain Followup column. Fix factorize() also coerce categorical (character) time-varying covariates - baseline (_bas) counterparts - factors levels fixed full data. Previously fixed treatment columns factorized, character time-varying covariate realise different level sets across bootstrap resamples raise “newdata provided match fitted model” (often bootstrapped hazard analyses larger samples smaller bootstrap.sample). Numeric time-varying covariates left unchanged.

title: SEQTaRget v1.4.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-31 Strip row-level vectors fastglm objects reduce weight.statistics memory usage use new internal function print coefficient table. Strip row-level vectors outcome models storing @outcome.model Fix clean_fastglm strip row-level vectors nested multinomial weight models longer store survival.curve ggplot object; regenerate demand via km_curve() Removed several local() wrappers made several code optimizations. Improved documentation datasets package. Implement check perfect separation fitting logistic regression models. Fixed bug make improvements internal.weights(). Removed three unused slots SEQopts(). Add alt text figures vignettes. Fixed SEQuential() time.col validation detecting repairing non-zero-indexed time. Add validation eligible.col values Add Paul Madley-Dowd co-author Add check overlapping time_varying.cols fixed.cols Add bounds validation numeric integer options SEQopts() Add check duplicate id/time combinations input data Add check treat.level values exist treatment column Add validation excused.cols flags Add validation followup.min/max ordering Add binary check outcome.col non-hazard analyses Add treat.level length validation multinomial non-multinomial analyses Add binary validation cense.eligible weight.eligible_cols Remove additional eligibility rows needed Amend defaults followup.min weight.lower -Inf 0 Fix bootstrapping risk difference risk ratio estimates use paired per-iteration estimates Optimizations use less RAM Fix duplicate scale_color_manual warning plot.subtitle label bug internal.plot() Run doseresponse ITT vignette chunks GitHub Actions Fix km_curve() returning list instead ggplot non-subgroup case Fix km_curve() subtitle condition Fix risk.comparison() CIs NA competing events Move selection.random expansion reduce peak memory usage Replace cbind() := expansion chain avoid intermediate copy Replace merge() data.table native join expansion data_list combine step Replace rbind weight construction copy+-place reduce peak memory Drop wt tmp columns immediately weight computed code paths Remove redundant setDF calls fast_model_matrix Free WDT bootstrap loop data.return FALSE Use match(TRUE, ...) instead (...)[1] find first switch/event per group Replace sapply loop single matrix multiply multinomial prediction Vectorise survival curve predictions single inline.pred call per treatment level Free result list extraction internal_survival.R reduce peak memory bootstrap Free analytic list subgroup loop SEQuential.R reduce peak memory survival curve computation Avoid copy() data_all construction free data list internal_survival.R reduce peak memory bootstrap Filter followup==0 adding trialID internal.survival avoid copying entire expanded dataset Trim base_DT prediction-needed columns replication internal.survival reduce peak memory Remove unnecessary copy(weight) model.data internal.weights since never modified -place Free baseDT bootstrap loop internal.survival reduce peak memory survival curve computation Fix multinomial.summary: replace vcov() fastglm $se field add missing Coefficient column prevent rbind mismatch Add test_coverage.R tests targeting uncovered code paths increase coverage Remove longer used variables dead code memory reduction optimizations
